race.name: Nordenskiöldsloppet
race.tagline: Two hundred and twenty kilometers above the Arctic Circle — the longest cross-country ski race on earth, unchanged since 1884.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple checkpoints with hot food, drinks, and wax service along the 220km route

climate.description: Temperatures range from -25°C to -5°C in mid-March above the Arctic Circle. Clear nights bring extreme cold, while daytime sun can soften snow. Skiers race through the full Arctic light cycle — starting in darkness, skiing through dawn, daylight, and potentially back into darkness.
challenges: Extreme cold during night sections, frostbite risk on exposed skin
30-hour effort demands sleep management and caloric planning
Wax selection across massive temperature swings between night and day
Arctic wind exposure on open frozen lakes and marshland
Limited daylight hours — headlamp skiing required for slower participants

course.primary: Point-to-point ultra-distance route through Arctic Lapland wilderness. Crosses frozen rivers, open marshes, and boreal forest. The terrain is predominantly flat to gently rolling, but the sheer distance and cold make it an extreme endurance test.
course.surface: Groomed classic tracks through wilderness, frozen lakes, and forest trails
course.grooming: Well-groomed given the extreme remoteness; snowmobile-set tracks through wilderness sections
features: World's longest cross-country ski race at 220km
Crosses the Arctic Circle during the route
Passages over frozen rivers and lakes
Sami cultural heritage along the route
30-hour time limit tests mental endurance as much as physical

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Perfect distance and scenery scores. The 220km ultra-distance is unmatched globally. Limited field size and remote Arctic location constrain accessibility and community scores, but the race's 140-year history and extreme format earn maximum prestige.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 5 with score < 75 caps at TIER 2

history.summary: Founded in 1884, Nordenskiöldsloppet is the world's longest cross-country ski race and one of the oldest competitive ski events still held. Named after Finnish-Swedish Arctic explorer Adolf Erik Nordenskiöld, the race commemorates the extreme skiing feats of Sami reindeer herders in Swedish Lapland. The event was sporadic in its early decades but has been held consistently in recent years, drawing adventurers and ultra-endurance athletes to Jokkmokk, a Sami cultural center above the Arctic Circle.
notable_facts: Founded in 1884 — predates even the Holmenkollen ski festival
Named after Adolf Erik Nordenskiöld, the first to navigate the Northeast Passage
At 220km, it is the longest cross-country ski race in the world
Jokkmokk hosts the famous Sami winter market, held since 1605
The 30-hour time limit means some skiers race through an entire Arctic night
Historically inspired by Sami reindeer herders' legendary long-distance skiing abilities

youtube_data.search_query: Nordenskiöldsloppet cross country ski race Jokkmokk to Vuollerim

videos.title: Nordenskiöldsloppet 2024. Jokkmokk till Njavve. Och tillbaka.
videos.description: Den 23 mars 2024 gick sjunde upplagan av Nordenskiöldsloppet i modern tid. Loppet följer en historisk bansträckning från 1884 och leder genom orörd vildmark norr om polcirkeln. Det blev ett extraordinärt skidäventyr över 220 km. Var med och skriv din egna hjältehistoria!
videos.curation_reason: Recent 2024 race coverage highlighting the historic route through untouched wilderness north of the Arctic Circle.

videos.title: A CROSS COUNTRY SKIER | From Training to Triumph: Inside Red Bull Nordenskiöldsloppet
videos.description:
In the early morning of March 26, 2022 Kristoffer Hjärtström stands on the starting line of his biggest challenge yet. He's about to start Red Bull Nordenskiöldsloppet, the longest and toughest cross-country ski race in existence. The race covers 220 kilometers in the arctic wilderness. His entire season has led up to this moment, and now he will find out if his painstaking training and preparations will have been enough.

The film is presented by True Story:
https://truestorysport.com/

Check out Kristoffer on Instagram and Youtube:
https://instagram.com/kriggminator
https://youtube.com/@kristofferhjartstrom

If you enjoyed this film and would like to help me make more projects like this, please consider donating: 
https://ko-fi.com/tobiasjohansson

Thank you for watching!

#crosscountryskiing #redbull

videos.curation_reason: Excellent skier-filmed POV documentary following a competitor through training and the 220km race with detailed first-person insights.

videos.title: Nordenskiöldsloppet 2025. The world’s longest and toughest ski race. The highlights.
videos.description:
This was Nordenskiöldsloppet 2025!

On March 29, 2025, the 8th edition of Nordenskiöldsloppet in modern times took place.
The race follows a historic route from 1884 and leads through untouched wilderness north of the Arctic Circle. It’s an extraordinary skiing adventure spanning 220 km, creating memories that last a lifetime.

Johan Lövgren from Vännäs SK and Sofia Lindberg from Robertsfors IK/Team Edux claimed victory in their respective races with strong performances! 🏆

Register now and write your own hero story.

www.nordenskioldsloppet.com

Video: Vertikal Films Sweden/ Nordenskiöldsloppet 

#Nordenskiöldsloppet #Nordenskiöldsloppet2025 #fischer #skickassics #livetoski #fischernordic #fischerski #xcskiing #xcskiinglife #vasaloppet #skirace #sweden #jokkmokk

videos.title: 220km on Cross Country Skis - Nordenskiöldsloppet 2019
videos.description:
Another year in the books. For the fourth time, 409 skiers gathered in Jokkmokk, right above the arctic circle, to take on the challenge of doing 220km on cross country skis. Here is how it looked!

Info:
https://www.nordenskioldsloppet.se/

Follow us on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/nordenskioldsloppet/
videos.curation_reason: Race coverage showing the scale of the 220km challenge with 409 participants in arctic conditions.
